interpretation: A textual graph is represented as nodes and edges with sequential textual attributes.
title: 2402.07630v3 retrieval-unit claim 001

interpretation: The retrieved evidence is a connected Prize-Collecting Steiner Tree subgraph rather than an isolated list of top-k triples.
title: 2402.07630v3 retrieval-unit claim 002

interpretation: The connected subgraph is represented twice for generation: as a learned GNN graph token and as flattened natural-language graph text.
title: 2402.07630v3 retrieval-unit claim 003

interpretation: The returned subgraph also serves as an explanation artifact identifying the graph evidence used for an answer.
title: 2402.07630v3 retrieval-unit claim 004

interpretation: Encode node and edge text with a pretrained language model and store the embeddings in a nearest-neighbor index.
title: 2402.07630v3 retrieval-unit claim 005

interpretation: Encode the query with the same model and select top-k nodes and edges by cosine similarity.
title: 2402.07630v3 retrieval-unit claim 006

interpretation: Assign ranked relevance prizes and solve a Prize-Collecting Steiner Tree objective that balances prizes against an adjustable per-edge subgraph-size cost.
title: 2402.07630v3 retrieval-unit claim 007

interpretation: Transform high-prize edges into virtual nodes when necessary so edge prizes can be handled by a node-prize PCST solver.
title: 2402.07630v3 retrieval-unit claim 008

interpretation: Encode the retrieved subgraph with a graph attention architecture and project its pooled representation into the LLM embedding space.
title: 2402.07630v3 retrieval-unit claim 009

interpretation: Semantically scored nodes and edges are the atomic retrieval candidates.
title: 2402.07630v3 retrieval-unit claim 010

interpretation: A query-conditioned connected subgraph is the final structured retrieval unit supplied to generation.
title: 2402.07630v3 retrieval-unit claim 011

interpretation: The textualized connected subgraph is an explicit prompt context, while its GNN encoding is a soft graph prompt.
title: 2402.07630v3 retrieval-unit claim 012

interpretation: The method contrasts its context-aware subgraph unit with earlier isolated node, edge, or triple retrieval.
title: 2402.07630v3 retrieval-unit claim 013

interpretation: Perform top-k cosine nearest-neighbor retrieval independently over node and edge embeddings.
title: 2402.07630v3 retrieval-unit claim 014

interpretation: Use PCST to retain relevant candidates plus bridge elements needed to connect them, while penalizing graph size.
title: 2402.07630v3 retrieval-unit claim 015

interpretation: Tune k and edge cost to control evidence coverage and prompt size; reported WebQSP performance peaks before very large k introduces distracting information.
title: 2402.07630v3 retrieval-unit claim 016

interpretation: Generate from the question, textualized subgraph, and trainable graph token while keeping the base LLM frozen in the prompt-tuning setting.
title: 2402.07630v3 retrieval-unit claim 017

interpretation: PCST imposes structural coherence by producing one connected evidence subgraph rather than independently selected facts.
title: 2402.07630v3 retrieval-unit claim 018

interpretation: A GNN pools structural information into a single graph token, while textualization preserves node and edge details for LLM reasoning.
title: 2402.07630v3 retrieval-unit claim 019

interpretation: This analysis classifies G-Retriever as query-time local subgraph selection, not corpus-wide thematic summarization, because it has no community-report stage.
title: 2402.07630v3 retrieval-unit claim 020

interpretation: Conversational questions and creative instructions over real-world textual graphs.
title: 2402.07630v3 retrieval-unit claim 021

interpretation: Commonsense stance reasoning over explanation graphs.
title: 2402.07630v3 retrieval-unit claim 022

interpretation: Scene-graph question answering requiring spatial and multi-step inference.
title: 2402.07630v3 retrieval-unit claim 023

interpretation: Multi-hop knowledge-graph question answering over Freebase neighborhoods.
title: 2402.07630v3 retrieval-unit claim 024

interpretation: Evidence-oriented answers that identify supporting graph nodes or edges.
title: 2402.07630v3 retrieval-unit claim 025

interpretation: GraphQA standardizes ExplaGraphs, SceneGraphs, and WebQSP into textual graph, question, and answer records.
title: 2402.07630v3 retrieval-unit claim 026

interpretation: Evaluate commonsense classification and scene QA with accuracy, and WebQSP knowledge-base QA with Hit@1.
title: 2402.07630v3 retrieval-unit claim 027

interpretation: Compare inference-only, frozen-LLM prompt tuning, and LoRA-tuned settings.
title: 2402.07630v3 retrieval-unit claim 028

interpretation: Manually inspect 100 SceneGraphs answers per method to check whether cited nodes and edges actually exist.
title: 2402.07630v3 retrieval-unit claim 029

interpretation: Ablate the graph encoder, projection, textualized graph, and retrieval components on WebQSP.
title: 2402.07630v3 retrieval-unit claim 030

interpretation: G-Retriever leads the reported results across three textual-graph domains in prompt-tuned and LoRA settings.
title: 2402.07630v3 retrieval-unit claim 031

interpretation: On SceneGraphs and WebQSP, retrieval sharply reduces prompt tokens, graph nodes, and training time.
title: 2402.07630v3 retrieval-unit claim 032

interpretation: Manual validation reports substantially more valid cited nodes, valid edges, and fully valid evidence graphs than graph prompt tuning without retrieval.
title: 2402.07630v3 retrieval-unit claim 033

interpretation: Ablations support complementarity between the GNN graph token and explicit textualized graph.
title: 2402.07630v3 retrieval-unit claim 034

interpretation: PCST retrieval achieves higher reported WebQSP retrieval accuracy than isolated top-k triple retrieval because it incorporates graph connectivity.
title: 2402.07630v3 retrieval-unit claim 035

interpretation: The retrieval component is static; the authors name trainable retrieval as future work.
title: 2402.07630v3 retrieval-unit claim 036

interpretation: Retrieval quality is sensitive to k: too few candidates omit evidence, while too many introduce distracting information.
title: 2402.07630v3 retrieval-unit claim 037

interpretation: Cosine similarity is adopted as a general fast choice, but the authors explicitly do not claim it is optimal.
title: 2402.07630v3 retrieval-unit claim 038

interpretation: Inference-only results reveal that adding an entire noisy textual graph can underperform answering from the question alone.
title: 2402.07630v3 retrieval-unit claim 039

interpretation: The evaluation uses three benchmark graph families and does not test document-to-graph extraction or corpus-wide global summarization.
title: 2402.07630v3 retrieval-unit claim 040

interpretation: SceneGraphs retrieval reduces average graph text from 1,396 to 235 tokens and training time from 123.1 to 86.8 minutes per epoch.
title: 2402.07630v3 retrieval-unit claim 041

interpretation: WebQSP retrieval reduces average graph text from 100,627 to 610 tokens, nodes from 1,371 to 18, and training time from 18.7 to 6.2 minutes per epoch.
title: 2402.07630v3 retrieval-unit claim 042

interpretation: PCST is solved using a near-linear-time approach after the edge-prize transformation.
title: 2402.07630v3 retrieval-unit claim 043

interpretation: The reported retrieval index is static; no incremental graph-update workflow is evaluated.
title: 2402.07630v3 retrieval-unit claim 044

interpretation: The authors frame the target as general question answering over real textual graphs rather than conventional graph classification or toy graph queries.
title: 2402.07630v3 retrieval-unit claim 045

interpretation: G-Retriever combines an LLM, graph encoder, and retrieval-augmented generation through indexing, retrieval, subgraph construction, and generation.
title: 2402.07630v3 retrieval-unit claim 046

interpretation: Node and edge texts are embedded, and the query retrieves top-k candidates by cosine similarity.
title: 2402.07630v3 retrieval-unit claim 047

interpretation: PCST selects a connected evidence subgraph by maximizing relevance prizes minus an edge-count cost.
title: 2402.07630v3 retrieval-unit claim 048

interpretation: Edge relevance is incorporated into node-prize PCST by replacing certain edges with prize-bearing virtual nodes.
title: 2402.07630v3 retrieval-unit claim 049

interpretation: Generation conditions a frozen LLM on both a learned graph token and a textualized version of the retrieved subgraph.
title: 2402.07630v3 retrieval-unit claim 050

interpretation: GraphQA spans commonsense stance reasoning, scene understanding, and multi-hop knowledge-base question answering.
title: 2402.07630v3 retrieval-unit claim 051

interpretation: G-Retriever reports the best scores among compared methods for all three datasets in the frozen prompt-tuning and LoRA settings.
title: 2402.07630v3 retrieval-unit claim 052

interpretation: Graph retrieval reduces WebQSP prompt nodes and tokens by about 99 percent and training time by 67 percent in the reported setup.
title: 2402.07630v3 retrieval-unit claim 053

interpretation: Manual evidence checking reports 62 percent fully valid supporting graphs for G-Retriever versus 8 percent for graph prompt tuning.
title: 2402.07630v3 retrieval-unit claim 054

interpretation: Removing the graph encoder or textualized graph causes much larger WebQSP drops than removing only the projection layer.
title: 2402.07630v3 retrieval-unit claim 055

interpretation: The number of initial semantic candidates must balance missing evidence against distracting graph content.
title: 2402.07630v3 retrieval-unit claim 056

interpretation: The authors distinguish PCST retrieval from isolated triple retrieval because it returns a connected subgraph containing bridge evidence.
title: 2402.07630v3 retrieval-unit claim 057

interpretation: The current retriever is static and the paper leaves trainable retrieval to future work.
title: 2402.07630v3 retrieval-unit claim 058

interpretation: G-Retriever is most directly applicable when a graph already exists and the question needs a compact local evidence subgraph; the paper does not establish global corpus summarization behavior.
title: 2402.07630v3 retrieval-unit claim 059
